summary: Ukrainian long‑range strikes hit a Moscow oil refinery, while Russian forces report UAV activity across multiple front‑line regions. Russia claims to have liberated the settlement of Novy Donbass in the Donetsk People’s Republic. Both sides continue to relocate strategic assets, including Russian Tu‑95 bombers.
key_points: Ukrainian forces struck a Moscow region oil refinery located about 500 km from the front, citing successful long‑range operations. (Source: @UkraineNow/81964)
Russian Ministry of Defence announced the “Center” formation liberated the settlement of Novy Donbass in the DNR. (Source: @tass_agency/380943)
Multiple UAV sightings were reported: a reactive UAV heading toward Sumy from the north, and another from Chernihiv region towards Oster. (Source: @kpszsu/65354, @kpszsu/65376)
A rocket was launched toward Donetsk near Novomykolayivka. (Source: @kpszsu/65359)
A military aircraft crashed in Khmelnytskyi region; Ukrainian sources suggest it may have been a MiG‑29. (Source: @tass_agency/380976)
Russia relocated at least three Tu‑95MS bombers from Engels airfield to Olenya, with reports of two additional bombers moving. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824)
Russia demonstrated the “Wave Dome Guarantee” system to jam Starlink satellites during tactical operations. (Source: @tass_agency/380964)

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian long‑range strikes hit a Moscow oil refinery, demonstrating extended strike capability. Russian forces claim to have liberated Novy Donbass in the DNR, indicating ongoing ground offensives. Both sides continue asset movements, including Russian Tu‑95 bomber relocations and increased UAV activity across fronts.

points: Ukrainian forces reported a successful strike on a Moscow region oil refinery located roughly 500 km from the front line, highlighting the use of long‑range precision weapons. (Source: @UkraineNow/81964)

Ukrainian officials noted increased UAV activity, including a reactive UAV approaching Sumy from the north and another UAV operating west of Chernihiv near Oster, suggesting heightened reconnaissance and strike missions. (Source: @kpszsu/65354, @kpszsu/65376)
A Ukrainian drone was observed heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the southeast, indicating continued aerial pressure on that sector. (Source: @kpszsu/65357)
Ukrainian intelligence reported a UAV moving north in Kherson region toward Velyka Oleksandrivka, reflecting expanded surveillance in the south‑west. (Source: @kpszsu/65353)
A Ukrainian UAV was sighted in Dnipropetrovsk region, north of Hirnytsky district, heading north‑east, showing operational reach across central Ukraine. (Source: @kpszsu/65363)

The Russian Ministry of Defence announced that the “Center” formation liberated the settlement of Novy Donbass in the Donetsk People’s Republic, indicating a localized offensive success. (Source: @tass_agency/380943)
A Russian military aircraft crashed in Khmelnytskyi region; Ukrainian sources suggest it may have been a MiG‑29, reflecting possible air losses. (Source: @tass_agency/380976)

Russia demonstrated the “Wave Dome Guarantee” anti‑satellite system capable of temporarily jamming Starlink satellites during tactical operations, showing advancement in electronic warfare. (Source: @tass_agency/380964)

At least three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were relocated from Engels to Olenya airfield, with additional reports of two more bombers moving, indicating strategic redeployment. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824)
A Russian rocket was launched toward Donetsk near Novomykolayivka, suggesting continued artillery or missile strikes in the contested area. (Source: @kpszsu/65359)
UAV activity was reported in Chernihiv region, with a drone operating west of the oblast near the settlement of Oster, heading on a variable course. (Source: @kpszsu/65354)
A reactive UAV was observed heading toward Sumy from the north, indicating potential strike or reconnaissance missions in the northeastern sector. (Source: @kpszsu/65376)
In Kharkiv region, a UAV was sighted heading toward Staryi Merchik, showing continued aerial operations in the east. (Source: @eRadarrua/96896)
A rocket launch was reported toward Donetsk near Novomykolayivka, reflecting ongoing artillery engagements on the eastern front. (Source: @kpszsu/65359)
A reactive UAV was observed moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv region, suggesting surveillance or strike intent north of the capital. (Source: @kpszsu/65347)
UAVs were reported in the north of Kherson heading toward Velyka Oleksandrivka, indicating north‑south axis activity. (Source: @kpszsu/65353)
Multiple UAVs were noted in the north of Kharkiv, with a north‑east course from the Hirnytsky district, showing persistent aerial presence. (Source: @kpszsu/65363)
UAV activity was reported in the south‑east direction over Kharkiv, launched by hostile tactical aviation, indicating pressure on that sector. (Source: @kpszsu/65342)
A UAV was observed from the Black Sea area heading toward Chornomorsk, reflecting maritime‑linked aerial operations in the south. (Source: @kpszsu/65374)
Ukrainian forces claim to have struck a Moscow oil refinery with long‑range missiles, demonstrating capability to target deep inside Russian territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/81964)
Russian anti‑satellite system “Wave Dome Guarantee” was shown to jam Starlink satellites during tactical operations, indicating electronic warfare escalation. (Source: @tass_agency/380964)
A Russian military aircraft, possibly a MiG‑29, crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, suggesting potential air losses for Russia. (Source: @tass_agency/380976)
Ukrainian UAVs were reported operating across multiple fronts, including north, east, and south, highlighting extensive use of drones in the air campaign. (Source: multiple @kpszsu posts)

key_developments: Ukrainian long‑range strike hit a Moscow oil refinery about 500 km from the front, showing extended strike capability. (Source: @UkraineNow/81964)
Russian Ministry of Defence announced the liberation of Novy Donbass in the DNR, indicating a localized ground gain. (Source: @tass_agency/380943)

Multiple UAV sightings across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Kherson regions suggest heightened aerial reconnaissance and strike activity. (Source: @kpszsu/65354, @kpszsu/65376, @kpszsu/65363, @kpszsu/65353)
A rocket launch toward Donetsk near Novomykolayivka reflects continued artillery pressure in the eastern sector. (Source: @kpszsu/65359)
A Russian military aircraft crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, with Ukrainian sources indicating it may have been a MiG‑29. (Source: @tass_agency/380976)
Russia relocated at least three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers from Engels to Olenya, signaling strategic asset movement. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824)
Russia demonstrated the “Wave Dome Guarantee” system to jam Starlink satellites during tactical operations, indicating advanced EW capabilities. (Source: @tass_agency/380964)
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian long‑range strike capabilities are extending the conflict into Russian interior targets, increasing pressure on Russian logistics. Russian UAV proliferation across multiple fronts enhances their reconnaissance and strike options, complicating Ukrainian defensive planning. The relocation of strategic bombers and deployment of anti‑satellite EW systems suggest Russia is reinforcing its strategic deterrent and attempting to degrade Ukrainian command‑and‑control networks.
sections.regional_response: Western partners at the G7 reaffirmed support for Ukraine, emphasizing additional Patriot missiles and energy assistance, though no direct operational details were provided. Russia’s claims of territorial gains and asset relocations aim to project resilience, while diplomatic channels remain active with discussions of potential meetings between leaders. Regional actors are closely monitoring the escalation of UAV activity and long‑range strikes.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate potential strike zones, especially near Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv. The reported crash of a possible MiG‑29 may indicate vulnerabilities in Russian air operations. The movement of Tu‑95 bombers to Olenya could affect NATO’s strategic calculations in the Baltic region. The effectiveness of Russia’s anti‑satellite system warrants further analysis for its impact on Ukrainian communications.
